title: Realm vs Client
description:
## Where Does My Web App Go?

You are integrating a web application that wants to delegate user login to Keycloak. In Keycloak's model, **where do you register that application** so it can use Keycloak for authentication?

options.text: As a **realm** — each application needs its own realm to authenticate users.
As a **user** inside the master realm, since the app must have an account to log in.
As a **client** within a realm — the realm is the isolated security domain (users, roles, clients), and a client represents an app/service that delegates login to Keycloak.
As a **role**, because the app's permissions define how it authenticates.

explanation: A **realm** is the isolated security domain that *contains* users, roles, and clients. A **client** represents a single application or service that wants Keycloak to authenticate its users (or that validates tokens). So you create the app as a **client inside the appropriate realm**. Option **a** confuses the container (realm) with the thing it contains (client) — you don't spin up a realm per app just to integrate one. Option **b** confuses an application with a human user account. Option **d** confuses a permission (role) with a registered application.

hints.title: Container vs occupant
hints.body: Think of the realm as the building and the client as one tenant application inside it. Many clients live in one realm.
